action: Read apps/api/scripts/archive-schema.sql RLS policies, apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma datasource, apps/api/src/lib/prisma.ts, apps/api/src/lib/archive.ts, apps/api/src/routes/labour.ts
result: RLS only exists on a separate Supabase 'archive' schema (backup mirror, ARCHIVE_DATABASE_URL, unset in .env) with fully permissive `WITH CHECK (true)` insert policies. Primary Neon DB has zero RLS. Prisma connects as `neondb_owner` (table-owning role), which Postgres never subjects to RLS even if it existed. archive.ts writes are fire-and-forget (try/catch swallowed, non-fatal).
verdict: DISPROVEN — RLS/DB-layer permission denial is not the cause

action: grep -rn registerBackgroundSync across apps/agent-native; read app/_layout.tsx
result: registerBackgroundSync() is called correctly in _layout.tsx:34 inside AppShell's useEffect on every app launch, independent of auth state.
verdict: DISPROVEN — background task registration is fine

action: Read NetworkBanner.tsx reconnect listener logic in full
result: NetInfo.addEventListener correctly flips wasOffline.current and calls sync() on a detected offline→online transition. BUT wasOffline.current starts false, and no AppState/mount-based sync exists anywhere in the app (grep for AppState: zero hits) — so a cold app launch while already online never triggers a sync, only a live transition during the session does. Only other auto-sync path is the 15-min-minimum background task (OS-throttled, especially unreliable on iOS).
verdict: CONFIRMED gap — 'auto sync whenever online' is narrower than advertised: it's 'auto sync on detected reconnect transition', not 'sync because online'

tool: Read + git diff + git show HEAD
action: Diffed NetworkBanner.tsx/backgroundSync.ts/three new.tsx screens against HEAD; read committed (deployed) apps/api/src/lib/auth.ts via git show HEAD
result: Uncommitted working-tree refactor strips all Clerk token calls from the agent-native client, replacing every api.post/patch/get(..., token) call with a literal ''. Client-side requireAgent relaxation (accepts empty token, maps to fixed DEVICE_AGENT_ID) exists ONLY in the uncommitted apps/api/src/lib/auth.ts. apps/agent-native/.env and eas.json (all 3 build profiles) hardcode EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE to the deployed https://carry-api-pink.vercel.app — which only runs committed code (last commit touching auth.ts: b07f2d5, predates this refactor). Deployed requireAgent still calls extractRoleFromJWT and returns 401 for any request with no/invalid Bearer token.
verdict: CONFIRMED — every API call from the refactored client (record POST, /uploads/sign, /uploads/patch-queued, sync flush) hits a live 401 wall on the real deployed backend

action: Read labour/new.tsx, properties/new.tsx, shops/new.tsx handleSubmit; sync.ts flushPendingUploads/flushPendingRecords
result: All three screens wrap api.post in a bare try{}catch{ /* offline */ } with zero NetInfo check and zero error-type inspection — any thrown error (network-down, timeout, 401, 500, validation 400) is treated identically as 'device is offline'. sync.ts's flushPendingUploads catches per-item and calls incrementUploadAttempts; flushPendingRecords Step 2 catches per-record with a bare comment '// Will retry next sync cycle' — same conflation.
verdict: CONFIRMED structural bug — misleading 'Saved Offline' messaging is not tied to actual connectivity at all, it's tied to 'the last attempt threw'

result: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=15000, single AbortController, no retry logic inside request() itself. A slow/weak connection either completes within 15s or hard-aborts with ApiError('Request timed out...', 408) — same generic catch-as-offline handling applies.
verdict: CONFIRMED — weak network manifests identically to a 401 or a hard-down connection: silently queued as 'offline', no distinguishing signal to the user

action: Re-read NetworkBanner.tsx useEffect for de-dup guard around sync()
result: No lock/ref guards concurrent sync() invocations. On flapping isInternetReachable (common on weak signal, since it's a periodic reachability probe not an instant signal), each offline→online blip re-triggers sync() with no de-dup, risking overlapping runFullSync() calls reading/mutating the same pending_uploads/pending_records rows concurrently.
verdict: CONFIRMED secondary risk — not the main bug, but a real gap for the 'weak network' half of the question

action: grep -rn 'attempts = 0' across apps/agent-native; grep requireAgent in apps/api/src/routes/uploads.ts
result: /uploads/sign and /uploads/patch-queued both gated by requireAgent (same 401 wall). MAX_ATTEMPTS=5 in sync.ts; zero code anywhere resets the attempts counter on pending_uploads. Once an upload hits 5 attempts it is permanently skipped by both flushPendingUploads and flushPendingRecords Step 1, which permanently blocks the parent pending_record's allReady check in Step 2 — the record can never submit and NetworkBanner's 'Retry' button (which just re-calls sync()) cannot unstick it.
verdict: CONFIRMED — any record-with-photo created while this auth mismatch is live and left running for 5 sync cycles (as little as ~5 app opens with connectivity, or one background-task cycle if it retries fast) becomes permanently stuck with no recovery path in the UI
